spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates that `caret-animation: manual` opens both transitions and keyframes — closing a knowledge gap that the spec wording doesn't make obvious.

interactive.rationale: Two inputs, one with a toggle that drives a transition, one with a continuous keyframe loop.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises one opt-out, two animation primitives; page proves it.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show Web Animations API (the third primitive), nor what happens when both transition and keyframes target caret-color simultaneously (spec composition order).

openQuestions.title: Add Web Animations API as third primitive
openQuestions.detail: Element.animate() is the JS counterpart. A small button that JS-animates caret-color via animate() would complete the trio and show that `caret-animation: manual` is the underlying gate for all three.

openQuestions.title: Composition order: transition + keyframes
openQuestions.detail: If you set both `transition: caret-color 1s` and a keyframe animation on caret-color, what wins? The spec composition rules apply — keyframes override transitions during their active period. Demonstrate.

summary: A small but useful concept that fills a knowledge gap. Add Web Animations API to make the trio complete and show composition order when both primitives target caret-color.
